BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The conditions in the morning are spring-like. The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase during the day.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust. The weather conditions from the late morning will give rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack.

In starting zones where no previous releases have taken place and on very steep slopes more small and medium-sized gliding avalanches and moist snow slides are possible as the penetration by moisture increases.
High altitudes and the high Alpine regions: The near-surface layers of the snowpack can be released in isolated cases by individual winter sport participants.

From late morning the weather will be partly cloudy. Backcountry tours should be started early and concluded timely.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Below approximately 3000 m: The snowpack is moist and its surface has a melt-freeze crust. The surface of the snowpack will already soften in the late morning.

As a consequence of mild temperatures and sleet the snowpack will settle at the weekend. This applies in particular at low and intermediate altitudes.

The high humditiy will give rise to moistening of the snowpack. This applies in particular on sunny slopes at intermediate and high altitudes, and in all aspects at low and intermediate altitudes.
Below approximately 2400 m only a little snow is lying on south facing slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Monday: Little snow will fall in some localities. The avalanche danger will persist.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The conditions in the morning are spring-like. The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase during the day.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust. The weather conditions from the late morning will give rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack.

In starting zones where no previous releases have taken place and on very steep slopes more small and medium-sized gliding avalanches and moist snow slides are possible as the penetration by moisture increases.
High altitudes and the high Alpine regions: The near-surface layers of the snowpack can be released in isolated cases by individual winter sport participants.

From late morning the weather will be partly cloudy. Backcountry tours should be started early and concluded timely.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Below approximately 3000 m: The snowpack is moist and its surface has a melt-freeze crust. The surface of the snowpack will already soften in the late morning.

As a consequence of mild temperatures and sleet the snowpack will settle at the weekend. This applies in particular at low and intermediate altitudes.

The high humditiy will give rise to moistening of the snowpack. This applies in particular on sunny slopes at intermediate and high altitudes, and in all aspects at low and intermediate altitudes.
Below approximately 2400 m only a little snow is lying on south facing slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Monday: Little snow will fall in some localities. The avalanche danger will persist.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase during the day.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The surface of the snowpack will only just freeze and will already soften in the late morning.
In particular on very steep slopes and in steep rocky terrain more small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized moist snow slides and avalanches are possible as the penetration by moisture increases. During the night as well, individual gliding avalanches are possible.

High altitudes and the high Alpine regions: The near-surface layers of the snowpack can be released in isolated cases by individual winter sport participants.
Naturally triggered avalanches and penetration of the old snow cover show the existence of a weak snowack.

Backcountry tours should be concluded timely.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The snowpack is moist and its surface has a melt-freeze crust.

The spring-like weather conditions as the day progresses will give rise to rapid moistening of the snowpack.
Below approximately 2400 m only a little snow is lying on south facing slopes. Above approximately 1600 m there is still a very large amount of snow on north facing slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Monday: Little rain will fall in some localities. The avalanche danger will persist.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase during the day.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The surface of the snowpack will only just freeze and will already soften in the late morning.
In particular on very steep slopes and in steep rocky terrain more small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized moist snow slides and avalanches are possible as the penetration by moisture increases. During the night as well, individual gliding avalanches are possible.

High altitudes and the high Alpine regions: The near-surface layers of the snowpack can be released in isolated cases by individual winter sport participants.
Naturally triggered avalanches and penetration of the old snow cover show the existence of a weak snowack.

Backcountry tours should be concluded timely.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The snowpack is moist and its surface has a melt-freeze crust.

The spring-like weather conditions as the day progresses will give rise to rapid moistening of the snowpack.
Below approximately 2400 m only a little snow is lying on south facing slopes. Above approximately 1600 m there is still a very large amount of snow on north facing slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Monday: Little rain will fall in some localities. The avalanche danger will persist.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be reduced in some case. More moist snow slides and avalanches are possible.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
In particular in the Prealps a partly overcast night. The surface of the snowpack will only just freeze.
From late morning the weather will be partly cloudy. The danger of moist and wet avalanches will already increase in the late morning.

In particular on very steep slopes more small and medium-sized moist and wet avalanches are possible as the moisture increases.

Backcountry tours should be started very early and concluded timely.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night was reduced in some cases.
The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust only at high altitudes and will soften earlier than the day before. This applies in particular below the tree line.

Below approximately 2200 m no snow is lying on south facing slopes.

Some rain will fall in particular in the Prealps. As a consequence of mild temperatures and high relative humidity the snowpack can not consolidate during the next few days.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Monday: Little rain will fall. The avalanche danger will persist.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be reduced in some case. More moist snow slides and avalanches are possible.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
In particular in the Prealps a partly overcast night. The surface of the snowpack will only just freeze.
From late morning the weather will be partly cloudy. The danger of moist and wet avalanches will already increase in the late morning.

In particular on very steep slopes more small and medium-sized moist and wet avalanches are possible as the moisture increases.

Backcountry tours should be started very early and concluded timely.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night was reduced in some cases.
The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust only at high altitudes and will soften earlier than the day before. This applies in particular below the tree line.

Below approximately 2200 m no snow is lying on south facing slopes.

Some rain will fall in particular in the Prealps. As a consequence of mild temperatures and high relative humidity the snowpack can not consolidate during the next few days.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Monday: Little rain will fall. The avalanche danger will persist.
